The July 13 release decoupled window and element scroll restoration, and it closes a class of navigation bugs common in dashboard apps.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Every dashboard app I have worked on has the same layout: a fixed sidebar, a sticky top bar, and a main content pane that scrolls independently of the window. The moment you add TanStack Router’s scroll restoration to that layout, you run into a coupling problem. Before 1.170.18, window scroll and element scroll restoration were handled as one unit. Navigate away from a deeply scrolled data table, come back, and the router might restore window scroll correctly while leaving your element at the top, or vice versa. The bug is subtle enough that most teams stop at “good enough” and never look closer.
The release on July 13 changed that. router-core: handle window and element scroll restoration independently is the commit that fixes it. It is a single, targeted change, but it resolves the root cause rather than papering over symptoms.
The Weaker Pattern Before 1.170
The pre-fix behavior treated every scroll-restoration event as a single pass. If your component used useElementScrollRestoration alongside the global ScrollRestoration component, the timing was shared. Here is what that looked like in a…
